mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and stable core inflation indicates robust demand without pricing pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL growth stays below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production rising above its 12-month moving average confirms strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which drives short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of supply chain disruption or when INDPRO growth decouples from corporate earnings.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in greater than 55% of high-inflation months due to growth optimism overriding rate fears.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, leading to margin compression and negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate increased construction activity and capital expenditure, which directly correlates with positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved economic sentiment.
failure_condition: Housing starts decouple from broader equity performance during periods of severe mortgage rate volatility, resulting in a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter signals economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets price in recession risks early, causing equity rallies on anticipated rate cuts despite sub-1% GDP growth in greater than 60% of instances.
